title: Can one command fix {n_in} broken rows?
premise_1s: a wall of visibly broken data + '{n_in} ROWS' stamp — will it get fixed?
task_definition: CHALLENGE: fix it all with ONE command
proof_plan: Build the messiest possible 10,000-row spreadsheet (synthetic), then give one Miller command one shot to clean it; record the pty session, wall time, and row counts; replay the real bytes on screen.

script.input: {n_in} rows. {removed} of them are clones. The spacing? Typed by a cat.
script.proof: One command gets one shot to fix all of it. Blink and you'll miss it.
script.output: {wall} seconds. Every clone deleted. {n_out} perfect rows left.
script.verdict: Flawless victory. But can it survive one MILLION rows? That's next.

capture.shell_line: mlr --csv clean-whitespace then uniq -a messy_contacts.csv > clean_contacts.csv && wc -l *.csv

description_note: Real experiment: the terminal output, timing, and row counts in this video come from an actual recorded run (proof ledger in the pipeline). Narration is AI text-to-speech.
